description.p: Sparkamp is an open-source, keyboard-centric audio player for Linux and GNOME inspired by the classic Winamp interface. It supports MP3, OGG, FLAC and other common audio formats, and includes a full-featured 10-band equalizer, a media library, visualizer plugins, and custom CSS skinning.
Launch with sparkamp for the GTK4 graphical interface or sparkamp --tui for the terminal (TUI) mode.

First stable release. Adds macOS DMG build, GTK skin system with 14-variable CSS theming, media library polish (mode-button toggle states, locked column widths, repeat/shuffle text labels), visualizer width stability across player states, and CI pipelines that produce both a Linux Flatpak and a macOS DMG from every commit to main.

GTK4 is now the default interface (launch with sparkamp); TUI is available via sparkamp --tui. Adds media library with background scanning, spectrum visualizer with configurable color zones and mirror mode, bar visualizer improvements, shuffle persistence, and numerous GTK fixes including jump-window search, scroll-to-row, and replace-mode autoplay.
Initial public release featuring GStreamer audio backend, GTK4 GUI, TUI mode, 10-band EQ with pre-amp, media library, visualizer plugins, and CSS skinning.
